name: DES-2A "Sandstorm"
description: The Sandstorm introduces electrostatic pre-separation into the sifting pipeline. Incoming regolith passes through a triboelectric charging tunnel where particle-particle collisions impart differential surface charges to ice-bearing grains versus dry silicates. Charged particles are then deflected by high-voltage plate arrays into separate streams before reaching the mechanical centrifugal drums, dramatically improving ice recovery even from sub-5% concentration regolith. The electrostatic stage adds minimal mass but requires 1.1 MW to maintain the charging fields and plate voltages. Total throughput climbs to 12 t/hr of raw regolith with 313 kg/hr water output. At 25 tonnes, the Sandstorm requires a medium-lift lander or dedicated cargo delivery to deploy.
